title: The Voyage of the Beagle
summaries: "The Voyage of the Beagle" by Charles Darwin is a book published in 1839, chronicling the five-year HMS Beagle expedition around the world. Part travel memoir and part scientific journal, it documents Darwin's keen observations of biology, geology, and anthropology across distant lands. Written as Darwin began developing his revolutionary theories on evolution, the book captures his experiences exploring South America and beyond, offering vivid accounts of places and discoveries that would shape scientific history. subjects: Voyages around the world -- History -- 19th century
